headline: SpaceX Is in the Nasdaq-100, Now Brace for Volatility
summary: SpaceX  trading on Tuesday won’t have much to do with space or AI fundamentals.  Elon Musk’s AI and space company is now in the Nasdaq-100, one of the best-known large-cap growth indexes that tracks the largest nonfinancial companies listed on Nasdaq.  Being fast-tracked into the index reflects just how important privately held frontier AI companies, including OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX before its June IPO, have become to the entire stock market.

headline: Meta says US states are seeking $1.4 trillion in penalties in August youth safety trial
summary: Meta Platforms said in a court filing on Monday that four states were seeking $1.4 trillion in penalties over accusations the company designed its Facebook and Instagram platforms to addict young users and misled ‌the public about their safety.  Meta put forward the figure in its response to the attorneys general's filings on how penalties should ‌be calculated if the states prevailed at trial.  The number, which has not previously been disclosed and is close to Meta’s market capitalization of around $1.5 trillion, comes ahead of an ​August trial in Oakland, California over the claims brought by California, Colorado, Kentucky and New Jersey against the company.

content.title: Meta says US states are seeking $1.4 trillion in penalties in August youth safety trial
content.summary: Meta Platforms said in a court filing on Monday that four states were seeking $1.4 trillion in penalties over accusations the company designed its Facebook and Instagram platforms to addict young users and misled ‌the public about their safety.  Meta put forward the figure in its response to the attorneys general's filings on how penalties should ‌be calculated if the states prevailed at trial.  The number, which has not previously been disclosed and is close to Meta’s market capitalization of around $1.5 trillion, comes ahead of an ​August trial in Oakland, California over the claims brought by California, Colorado, Kentucky and New Jersey against the company.

headline: 3 Internet Stocks on Our Watchlist
summary: Whether it be online shopping or social media, secular forces are propelling consumer internet businesses forward. But it’s not all sunshine and rainbows as consumer purchasing power can make or break demand. Unfortunately, the market seems to believe stormy skies are ahead as the industry has shed 9.9% over the past six months. This performance is a stark contrast from the S&P 500’s 8% gain.

headline: Nigeria to investigate tech firms over news content use
summary: Nigerian President Bola Tinubu has directed the country's competition regulator to investigate major technology companies ‌over alleged anti-competitive practices and unauthorised use of news content, the ‌Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission said late on Monday. The FCCPC said the inquiry would ​examine complaints by Nigerian media groups against companies including Meta, Alphabet, X and generative artificial intelligence platforms operating in Nigeria. The complaint was submitted by the Nigerian Press Organisation, which represents newspaper owners, journalists' unions, broadcasters and online publishers.

content.title: Nigeria to investigate tech firms over news content use
content.summary: Nigerian President Bola Tinubu has directed the country's competition regulator to investigate major technology companies ‌over alleged anti-competitive practices and unauthorised use of news content, the ‌Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission said late on Monday. The FCCPC said the inquiry would ​examine complaints by Nigerian media groups against companies including Meta, Alphabet, X and generative artificial intelligence platforms operating in Nigeria. The complaint was submitted by the Nigerian Press Organisation, which represents newspaper owners, journalists' unions, broadcasters and online publishers.

headline: Amazon aims to raise $25 billion from bond sale, Bloomberg News reports
summary: Amazon.com is looking to raise at least $25 billion through a U.S. dollar bond sale, Bloomberg ‌News reported on Tuesday, in the company's latest push ‌to fund its hefty AI investments.  Tech companies have been tapping debt markets and ​launching equity sales to fund their costly AI infrastructure build-out.  Big Tech, including Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft and Meta, are expected to spend more than $700 billion on AI this year.

Big Tech, including Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft and Meta, are expected to spend more than $700 billion on AI this year.

headline: OpenAI Wants a $1 Trillion Valuation. But College Students Are Testing At The Level Of 10-Year-Olds.
summary: Sam Altman wants Wall Street to value OpenAI at $1 trillion. Meanwhile, 14% of US college students are reading at or below the level of a 10-year-old, according to the OECD. Those two facts, sitting side by side, may explain why the most anticipated technology IPO in a generation just got pushed into 2027. OpenAI ... OpenAI Wants a $1 Trillion Valuation. But College Students Are Testing At The Level Of 10-Year-Olds.

headline: Is Google Cloud Officially Driving the Train?
summary: Let’s be honest. When a stock you own doubles, you feel like a genius. When it’s a behemoth like Alphabet (GOOGL), and it returns +105% in a year, you might wonder if you’ve misread the chart. You haven’t. Over the past 12 months, while the S&P 500 gained a respectable 21%, Alphabet left it in the dust. It also trounced its mega-cap peers; Microsoft (MSFT) stock returned -21.9%, and Meta Platforms (META) returned -16.2% over the same period.

content.title: Is Google Cloud Officially Driving the Train?
content.summary: Let’s be honest. When a stock you own doubles, you feel like a genius. When it’s a behemoth like Alphabet (GOOGL), and it returns +105% in a year, you might wonder if you’ve misread the chart. You haven’t. Over the past 12 months, while the S&P 500 gained a respectable 21%, Alphabet left it in the dust. It also trounced its mega-cap peers; Microsoft (MSFT) stock returned -21.9%, and Meta Platforms (META) returned -16.2% over the same period.

headline: Amazon Readies $25 Billion Bond With AI Costs Rising
summary: Amazon is reportedly readying a bond offering of at least $25 billion, as the tech giant continues to plow investments into AI-related infrastructure. headline: Amazon aims to raise $25 billion from bond sale, source says
summary: Amazon.com is looking to raise at least $25 billion through a U.S. dollar bond sale, ‌a source familiar with the matter told Reuters on Tuesday, in the ‌company's latest push to fund its hefty AI investments.  Tech companies have been tapping debt markets and launching ​equity sales to fund their costly AI infrastructure build-out.  Big Tech, including Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft and Meta, are expected to spend more than $700 billion on AI this year.

headline: Who will be the AI trade's real winners: The makers vs. the takers?
summary: Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) released preliminary second quarter earnings results on Tuesday, reporting a 19-fold profit jump from a year ago, amounting to $58 billion. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman welcomes Yahoo Finance Senior Business Reporter Ines Ferré and Interactive Brokers chief strategist Steve Sosnick to help break down whether Samsung's breakout numbers still weren't enough to impress Wall Street investors and what it signifies for the broader chip space.

content.title: Who will be the AI trade's real winners: The makers vs. the takers?
content.description:
Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) released preliminary second quarter earnings results on Tuesday, reporting a 19-fold profit jump from a year ago, amounting to $58 billion.

Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman welcomes Yahoo Finance Senior Business Reporter Ines Ferré and Interactive Brokers chief strategist Steve Sosnick to help break down whether Samsung's breakout numbers still weren't enough to impress Wall Street investors and what it signifies for the broader chip space.

headline: Amazon Stocks Climbs As AI Push Drives $25 Billion Bond Sale
summary: Amazon stock climbed Tuesday, following reports the tech giant is raising at least $25 billion through a bond offering to support its big investments into AI-related infrastructure.  The Seattle-based Amazon is raising the funds through an eight-part bond sale, according to reports in Bloomberg and the Wall Street Journal.  Amazon disclosed plans for the bond sale in regulatory filings Tuesday but did not disclose the amount.

Amazon disclosed plans for the bond sale in regulatory filings Tuesday but did not disclose the amount.

headline: META Stock Pops as Meta Platforms Launches AI Image Model
summary: Meta stock rallies as the multinational rolls out an AI image model dubbed Muse Image. Here’s why the launch is bullish for META shares.

headline: Meta faces potential $1.4 trillion in penalties
summary: In a child safety case, four U.S. states are seeking combined damages that the company estimates would be close to its market cap, per Reuters.

content.title: Meta faces potential $1.4 trillion in penalties
content.summary: In a child safety case, four U.S. states are seeking combined damages that the company estimates would be close to its market cap, per Reuters.

thumbnail.caption: The incident marks at least the fourth alleged bias-related event at a Meta or Facebook construction site in the last two years.

headline: Tech used to be a mecca for young talent—now, AI start-ups are hiring fewer entry-level talent in favor of older workers with top degrees
summary: Tech companies like Meta built a reputation for snatching talent before they’re out of college. But now, new AI start-ups are turning away young workers.

content.title: Tech used to be a mecca for young talent—now, AI start-ups are hiring fewer entry-level talent in favor of older workers with top degrees
